title: O Tan-Faced Prairie Boy
content:
O tan-faced prairie-boy!
Before you came to camp, came many a welcome gift;
Praises and presents came, and nourishing food - till at last, among the recruits,
You came, taciturn, with nothing to give - we but look'd on each other,
When lo! more than all the gifts of the world, you gave me.
meaning:
Walt Whitman's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "O Tan-Faced Prairie Boy"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
